name: Hamel Husain
bio: Hamel Husain is a machine learning engineer with over 20 years of experience, currently an independent consultant at Parlance Labs. He previously held applied ML roles at GitHub (2017-2022) and Airbnb (2016-2017), and earlier at DataRobot. At GitHub he led CodeSearchNet, a large-scale benchmark for semantic code search that laid groundwork later used by OpenAI for code understanding models. He co-created nbdev (5.3k stars), a literate-programming system built on Jupyter Notebooks, and fastpages (3.6k stars), an open-source notebook blogging platform. In January 2026 he published 'Why I Stopped Using nbdev', explaining how AI coding assistants changed the trade-offs of literate programming. Husain now focuses on AI evaluation methodology, co-teaching the 'AI Evals for Engineers & PMs' course on Maven with Shreya Shankar, with over 3,000 students from 500+ companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.

timeline.event: Published blog post 'Evals Skills for Coding Agents' on hamel.dev
Published 'Why I Stopped Using nbdev', explaining how AI coding tools changed literate programming trade-offs
Published 'LLM Evals: Everything You Need to Know' comprehensive FAQ on hamel.dev
Published 'Selecting The Right AI Evals Tool' on hamel.dev
Appeared on Lenny's Podcast with Shreya Shankar discussing why AI evals are the hottest new skill for product builders
Published 'Stop Saying RAG Is Dead' blog post
Published blog post on Inspect AI, an OSS Python library for LLM evals
Appeared on TWIML AI Podcast #694: 'Building Real-World LLM Products with Fine-Tuning and More'
Published 'Your AI Product Needs Evals' foundational blog post on AI evaluation methodology
Helped lead a complete rewrite of nbdev (v2) with fast.ai
Presented fastpages at JupyterCon 2020 and released the notebook blogging system
Led launch of CodeSearchNet, a large-scale benchmark and dataset for semantic code search at GitHub
Gave talks on 'Semantic Search at GitHub' and 'Natural Language Code Search With Kubeflow' at KubeCon 2018
Joined GitHub as Staff Machine Learning Engineer, began work on code representation learning
Joined Airbnb as Data Scientist, discovered critical data-leakage bug in growth marketing model, created blueprints that informed BigHead ML platform
Started career building credit risk models at a bank, entering ML before the term was common

key_contributions.title: nbdev
key_contributions.description: Co-created with fast.ai the literate programming framework 'Create delightful software with Jupyter Notebooks'. 5.3k stars on GitHub. Led the v2 rewrite in 2022.

key_contributions.title: fastpages
key_contributions.description: Open-source blogging platform with enhanced Jupyter Notebook support, enabling data scientists to publish notebooks as blog posts. 3.6k stars (archived).

key_contributions.title: CodeSearchNet
key_contributions.description: Large-scale benchmark and dataset for semantic code search, enabling representation learning of code across multiple programming languages. 2.4k stars. Led at GitHub.

key_contributions.title: AI Evals for Engineers & PMs (Maven course)
key_contributions.description: Co-taught with Shreya Shankar, enrolled 3,000+ students from 500+ companies including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. Teaches systematic AI product evaluation methodology.

key_contributions.title: evals-skills
key_contributions.description: Skills resource complementing the AI Evals course, providing practical evaluation templates and examples. 891 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: claude-review-loop
key_contributions.description: Automated code review tool using Claude for iterative review feedback. 590 stars on GitHub.

key_contributions.title: 'Your AI Product Needs Evals' (blog post)
key_contributions.description: Influential blog post establishing the case that unsuccessful AI products almost always share a common root cause: failure to create robust evaluation systems.

quotes.text: Unsuccessful products almost always share a common root cause: a failure to create robust evaluation systems.
quotes.source: Your AI Product Needs Evals (blog post)

quotes.text: Don't rely on generic evaluation frameworks to measure the quality of your AI. Instead, create an evaluation system specific to your problem.
quotes.source: Your AI Product Needs Evals (blog post)

quotes.text: Like software engineering, success with AI hinges on how fast you can iterate.
quotes.source: Your AI Product Needs Evals (blog post)

quotes.text: The abuse of generic metrics is endemic. Many eval vendors promote off the shelf metrics, which ensnare engineers into superfluous tasks.
quotes.source: LLM Evals: Everything You Need to Know

quotes.text: I write software to solve problems, not to write code. I want to work in an environment where AI has the highest chance of success.
99% of the labor involved with fine-tuning is assembling high-quality data that covers your AI product's surface area.
quotes.source: Your AI Product Needs Evals (blog post)
